title: Audit Interface
caption: A public learning interface is proposed around the underlying evaluation protocol.

title: Reverse Turing Test
caption: The question is not whether machines can fool humans. It is whether humans can still detect machine cultural error.

title: AI Claim Card
caption: The model presents a plausible claim that may be culturally false.

title: Compare / Ground / Classify
caption: The user inspects an output, opens its references, classifies the failure, and reviews the reasoning.

title: Grounding Pass
caption: Cultural claims must pass through evidence before becoming trusted.

title: Spectral Input
caption: Sonic failures can become part of the hallucination test.

title: Audit Record
caption: A result combines classification, evidence, confidence, and an explicit note about interpretive limits.

title: Failure Taxonomy
caption: Errors are grouped by omission, substitution, flattening, fabrication, and loss of culturally specific structure.

title: Relation to Localization Gap / Meaning Stack
caption: The audit becomes a defensive interface.

title: The Human Auditor
caption: The final checkpoint is still human discernment.
